definition: A careful examination of a product, machine or process to check its condition or quality.

sentences.text: The operator performed a visual _____ before starting the production line.
The quality _____ found a small crack on the welded part.

definition: Work performed to keep equipment in good condition and prevent or repair failures.

sentences.text: The _____ team replaced the damaged proximity sensor.
Preventive _____ helps reduce unexpected machine failures.

definition: The amount of light produced by or reflected from a surface, screen or image.

sentences.text: The camera _____ was increased to make the part easier to detect.
Incorrect _____ settings can cause unreliable inspection results.
The operator adjusted the screen _____ before checking the image.

definition: The straight-line distance across the center of a circle or cylindrical object.

sentences.text: The operator measured the _____ of the shaft with a digital caliper.
The hole _____ must remain within the specified tolerance.
A larger pipe _____ allows more material to flow through the system.

definition: To continue giving someone the latest information about a situation or process.

sentences.text: Please _____ on the progress.

Can you _____ while I am away?

definition: Intended to stop a problem, failure or illness before it happens.

sentences.text: The maintenance team performs _____ checks every Friday.

The company introduced a _____ maintenance plan to reduce unexpected downtime.

definition: A person who asks questions during an interview, especially for a job.

sentences.text: The _____ asked me to describe a difficult problem I had solved.
I maintained eye contact with the _____ and answered each question clearly.
At the end of the meeting, the _____ explained the next stage of the hiring process.

definition: Having the skills, experience or education needed for a particular job or activity.

sentences.text: She is highly _____ for the engineering position because of her technical experience.
Only _____ candidates will be invited to the final interview.
The company is looking for a _____ technician who can work independently.

definition: A person who formally applies for a job, course or opportunity.

sentences.text: Each _____ must submit a CV and a short cover letter.
The company invited the strongest _____ to a second interview.
As an _____, you should research the company before the interview.

definition: The act of agreeing to receive, approve or admit something or someone.

sentences.text: She received an _____ email from the university yesterday.

Final _____ depends on completing all required documents.

definition: An approximate calculation or judgment of a value, cost, time or amount.

sentences.text: The engineer prepared an _____ of the time needed for commissioning.

Can you give me an _____ of when the repair will be finished?

definition: A feeling of worry or an issue that requires attention.

sentences.text: Safety is our main _____ before restarting the machine.

Please contact me if you have any _____ about the new procedure.

definition: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something said before.

sentences.text: The machine passed the first test; _____, the safety check still needs to be completed.

definition: The process of returning to a normal, healthy or successful condition after a problem or difficult period.

sentences.text: Her _____ after the operation was faster than expected.
The company is showing signs of _____ after a difficult year.
Getting enough rest is important for a full _____.

definition: Used to say that one thing replaces another or is chosen as an alternative.

sentences.text: We decided to stay home _____ of going out.
The bus was full, so I took the train _____.
Use this cable _____ because the original one is damaged.

definition: Fair, sensible or not more than what is acceptable in a particular situation.

sentences.text: The hotel offered a clean room at a _____ price.
Your request sounds _____, so I think the manager will accept it.
We need a _____ amount of time to complete the task safely.

definition: Unwilling to change an opinion, decision or way of behaving even when others disagree.

sentences.text: He is too _____ to admit that he made a mistake.

definition: Moving or handling things awkwardly and often dropping or breaking them.

sentences.text: I felt _____ and dropped my phone on the floor.

definition: Very careful about choosing things and difficult to satisfy.

sentences.text: My younger brother is very _____ about food.

definition: Too interested in other people's private lives or personal information.

sentences.text: Our _____ neighbor always asks personal questions.
I do not want to sound _____, but why did you leave your job?
He became _____ and read the message on her screen.

definition: To understand something or find the answer to a problem after thinking about it.

sentences.text: I am trying to _____ why the application keeps closing.

Can you _____ the best way to solve this problem?

definition: To use all of something so that none of it is left.

sentences.text: We _____ milk this morning, so I went to the store.
The machine stopped because it had _____ raw material.
Be careful not to _____ time before the test ends.

definition: To deal with a situation, task or problem in a capable and controlled way.

sentences.text: She knows how to _____ difficult customers calmly.
Can you _____ this task while I check the machine?
He _____ the unexpected problem better than I expected.

definition: To open or release something that has been tied, buttoned, locked or otherwise secured.

sentences.text: Please _____ your seat belt only after the warning light turns off.

definition: Related to the home, family life or activities within a particular country.

sentences.text: The airport has separate terminals for international and _____ flights.

definition: A traditional term for a female flight attendant who serves and assists passengers on an aircraft.

sentences.text: The _____ showed the passengers where to store their bags.

definition: A document that lists goods or services provided and states the amount that must be paid.

sentences.text: The supplier emailed the _____ after delivering the equipment.
Please check the _____ before approving the payment.
The customer noticed an incorrect amount on the _____.

definition: A thick bed covering made by sewing together layers of fabric, often with decorative patterns.

sentences.text: She placed a warm _____ over the bed before winter.

definition: A large road vehicle used for carrying goods, especially in British English.

sentences.text: A delivery _____ was waiting outside the factory gate.
The _____ carried heavy equipment across the country.
Traffic slowed down after a _____ broke down on the motorway.

definition: To find or meet someone or something unexpectedly while doing something else.

sentences.text: I _____ an old photo while cleaning my desk.
You may _____ unfamiliar terms while reading the manual.

definition: To continue to be in the same state, condition or place without changing.

sentences.text: Please _____ calm while the technician checks the system.
Several questions _____ unanswered after the meeting.

definition: Knowing about or understanding a particular fact, situation or problem.

sentences.text: All employees should be _____ of the safety rules.

definition: To successfully reach a goal or obtain a desired result through effort.

sentences.text: The team worked together to _____ the production target.
She studied every evening to _____ a higher exam score.

definition: A way of dealing with a problem, task or situation.

sentences.text: We need a different _____ to solve this repeated fault.

The team discussed the best _____ for reducing cycle time.

definition: To make something better or to become better.

sentences.text: Regular practice will _____ your speaking skills.

definition: To need something or make something necessary.

sentences.text: This position may _____ occasional travel to customer sites.
The repair will _____ several new components.
Most engineering roles _____ strong problem-solving skills.

definition: To include someone or something as a necessary part of an activity or situation.

sentences.text: The project will _____ programming three industrial robots.
This role may _____ direct communication with customers.
Solving the issue could _____ replacing the damaged cable.

definition: To happen, especially unexpectedly or at a particular time.

sentences.text: The fault tends to _____ when the motor reaches full speed.
